findings.message: New public endpoint `count_items` (GET /items/count) lacks a docstring. All sibling public route handlers in this codebase (e.g. `get_items`, `heartbeat`, `health`, `metrics`) document purpose and contract; this one is undocumented.
findings.suggestion: Add a docstring describing what the endpoint returns and its intended use, e.g. """Return the total count of items as {\"count\": int}. Convenience for clients that only need the size."""

findings.message: New endpoint `GET /items/count` is not listed in README.md's `## Endpoints` section, which enumerates the other public endpoints (`/heartbeat`, `/health`, `/metrics`, `/items`). The README will become stale on merge.
findings.suggestion: Add a bullet under README.md `## Endpoints`: `- `GET /items/count` — count of items in the store`.

findings.message: `count_items` returns an untyped `dict` and declares no `response_model`, unlike the sibling `get_items` which uses `response_model=list[ItemRead]`. The OpenAPI schema will lack a documented response shape for this endpoint, and `openapi.json` (committed per README's CI/CD section) will need regeneration.
findings.suggestion: Define a small response schema (e.g. `class ItemCount(BaseModel): count: int`) and annotate `-> ItemCount` with `@router.get("/items/count", response_model=ItemCount)`, then run `task openapi:export` and commit the refreshed `openapi.json`.
